entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-17 07:23AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘The way he plays is very dangerous’: Swans fans swooning over ‘The Chad’
  • ‘Competitor’ Lleyton Hewitt enshrined into Tennis Hall of Fame
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘Contagion effect’: Students choose unscored VCE in record numbers
  • Breust, Hawks fly high to down the Eagles
  • Pressure mounts on Griffin before Dragons board meeting
  • Ukrainian operated cargo plane carrying ‘dangerous material’ crashes in Greece
  • ‘Makes more sense’: Investors back ANZ play for Suncorp assets as MYOB deal put on ice
  • Victoria to hire 400-strong hospital strike force
  • Sydney set for record rainfall despite sunny weekend
  • Same old Tigers, warts and all, as depleted Panthers dash fairytale
  • ‘Up for shifting’: Greens seek guarantee 43 per cent emissions reduction target is a floor
  • Best of cartoons, July 17, 2022
  • Mortgages in retirement triple, outright ownership halves for most age groups
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 16, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • ‘Highly infectious’ COVID strains worry leaders as pandemic leave pay returns
  • ‘People aren’t following rules’: Plan to unleash dogs on Sydney beaches splits locals
  • ‘Dark, dingy and losing its character’: Can Chinatown’s decline be turned around?
  • ‘Major overhaul’: One-fifth of selective school places to go to disadvantaged students
  • Court overturns heritage listing on Sydney building, paving way for demolition
  • COVID-19 fine withdrawn in test case that could see thousands more torn up
  • Losing our religion and finding a church of love and respect
  • Three charged with murder over stabbing death of teen at Royal Easter Show
  • New Zealand extends fuel excise cut until next year in bid to help rising cost of living
  • Thousands forced from homes as fires rage across Europe
  • Biden says he confronted Saudi Crown Prince over Khashoggi. How true is that?
  • Inside the Ukraine war crimes investigation, Part 2: Torture cells
  • Netflix might have saved the royals, says Andrew Hastie
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Torture Cell
  • Washed-up white whale is not Migaloo, experts confirm
  • COVID mantra of ‘personal responsibility’ is fine – in theory
  • Queensland cracks automotive milestone – 10,000 electric vehicles
  • London rail boss backs Andrews’ vision for Suburban Rail Loop
  • Would-be travellers still trying to recoup trips axed at start of pandemic
  • Blustery conditions ease but blizzard on the way for alpine areas
  • Too many flies? Horse neigh sayers block new houses near country racetrack
  • From the Archives, 1977: Victoria’s ‘abalone war’ with NSW poachers
  • The Age photos of the week, July 15, 2022
  • The jailhouse lawyer who helped set Jason Roberts free
  • Melbourne’s ‘red wall’ of shrinking suburbs revealed
  • The future of libraries, or haven for piracy? The case of the Internet Archive
  • Big dose of Dolly gives licence to laugh at inequity and sexism
  • Review: Time as Revelator embraces jagged edge of classical music
  • The 17 TV shows we’re still looking forward to in 2022
  • Grace, fun and technical prowess showcased in curiously hybrid ballet event
  • Even stranger things: how Hollywood is fast-tracking bright young stars
  • He launched the careers of Kylie, Delta and Margot. Do you know his name?
  • Joanna Lumley: ‘Even when my name was well known, my bank balance was still red’
  • Beyond Bluey: Does Australian children’s TV have a future?
  • ‘I imagined she was one of the Hanson brothers’: Jonathan Van Ness on their first kiss
  • Mauricio Umansky arrives in Sydney without his Beverly Hills housewife
  • Fed up with the chore wars, grumpy women are now politically dangerous
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia set to renew ground assaults, analysts say, as strikes pelt east

  • Outcry over U.S. visa delays after Kenyan sprinter nearly misses race

  • White House says Russian officials visited Iran twice to examine drones

  • Intersex youth are also hurt by anti-trans laws, advocates say

  • Facing trial, Bannon vows to go ‘medieval,’ but judge says meh
  • Democrats use Pentagon policy bill to close loopholes Trump exposed
  • Gas prices may surge again ahead of midterm elections
  • ‘Soulframe’ is the ‘Warframe’ creators’ follow-up to their decade-spanning hit
  • The best couch co-op games to play with friends
  • These robots were trained on AI. They became racist and sexist.

  • Nobody Knows How Long Inflation Will Last. That’s Life.

  • Even Senseless Assassins Can Get Lucky Once

  • Why India’s World-Beating Growth Isn’t Creating Jobs
  • Biden’s fist bump with MBS was a crass betrayal

  • These questions still need to be answered about Bijan Ghaisar’s death
  • The Wall Street Journal’s editorial shame
  • A question-spangled banner
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 17 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-17 07:12AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Utility bills, hospital visit records can be used as proof of residency in Hong Kong to qualify for HK$5,000 voucher scheme, finance chief says
  • Five areas where John Lee can improve the lives of Hong Kong youth
  • No smartphone, no entry? Isolated by Covid-19 rules, Hong Kong’s elderly dread changes to ‘Leave Home Safe’ mobile app
  • Hong Kong’s prosecutors will not bow to intimidation amid threat of US sanctions
  • Patriotic Education Centre to launch classes for Hong Kong students from September
  • Woman arrested for allegedly duping 22 residents out of HK$130,000 by offering fake discounted tickets to Hong Kong Disneyland
  • Macau extends coronavirus lockdown to next Friday, gives hard-hit businesses fresh round of financial aid
  • Hong Kong youth minister says she will not rule out bringing back ‘very progressive’ appointment system to pick district councillors
  • Sri Lanka turmoil highlights risk for Chinese firms investing overseas
  • Military drills a sign of Beijing’s increasingly aggressive stance towards Taiwan, analysts say
  • Ending Australia coal ban would put China ties back on track, minister says
  • Chinese diplomats flock to Africa in response to Western charm offensive
  • Chinese foreign minister criticises US ‘meddling’ in Middle East as Joe Biden continues tour of region
  • Scientists urge people to take second Covid vaccine booster if offered as Omicron continues to spread
  • Why China, unlike Russia, won’t be drawn on civil unrest in Central Asia
  • More cities across China order partial Covid lockdowns
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Government rejected Jho Low’s US$337 million offer to settle case, report says
  • Malaysians throng Japanese Bon Odori dance festival, defy Muslim groups’ stay away call
  • Was Indonesian freedom fighter Kapitan Pattimura actually a Muslim? Conspiracy highlights rising historical revisionism
  • Marcos misinterprets the cause of the Philippines’ food crisis. It was decentralisation, not weak leadership
  • As Malaysia’s parliament resumes, will it be Najib Razak’s last meeting?
  • Thailand lures wealthy foreigners with land ownership offer
  • G20 in Indonesia agrees on food security, but war prevents formal communique
  • Malaysian assets at risk globally as heirs of sultan, who are Philippine nationals, claim US$15 billion award
  • 9 civilians shot dead in Indonesia’s restive Papua, days after protests over new law that will see 5 provinces, instead of current 2
  • War-torn Syrian town springs back to life with Jackie Chan-produced film shoot
  • Will Biden’s fist-bump with Saudi crown prince MBS dent US president’s image as human rights defender?
  • Britain’s Duchess Camilla celebrates milestone birthday – future ‘Queen Consort’ is 75
  • Biden ends trip with US-Saudi relations on the mend but few other wins
  • Ukraine athlete favourite to win gold at world championships
  • Hundreds die as heatwave grips Europe and temperatures soar above 40ºC
  • US: 6 dead in Montana 21-car pile up after windy dust storm with ‘zero visibility’
  • ‘No vacuum for China, Russia, Iran’ to fill in Middle East, says Biden at Arab summit
  • Russia pounds Ukraine’s cities, kills more civilians, as it steps up operations
  • Amid economic slowdown, green bonds can help Southeast Asia stay on road to net zero in climate fight
  • Climate change a far greater threat to global economy than Covid-19 pandemic amid rising intensity of heatwaves and floods, Swiss Re says
  • Hong Kong hotelier hopes for restriction-free travel as industry slowly returns to pre-pandemic heyday across the world
  • How HSBC laundered money for Mexican drug cartels, paid a US$1.9b fine and hardly anyone noticed
  • Roller skating entrepreneur talks about finding her tribe in Hong Kong, after growing up in South Africa and being a safari guide
  • The best ice cream in Hong Kong and its little-known history in the city
  • Why are there so many artisan coffee shops opening in Hong Kong? How city’s drinking habits have evolved
  • Hong Kong ‘demon chef’ Alvin Leung on food art, life and death, heaven and hell, and his new menu The Masterpieces
  • Best mountain running and climbing films free on YouTube from Touching The Void to Project Endless
  • Likened to Hollywood hit Love Story, this film beat Jackie Chan, Jet Li at the Hong Kong box office in 1993
  • WNBA players are the next big fashion icons, styled in Dior and streetwear for events and photographed in trending pregame outfits
  • Nothing Phone 1 review: iPhone lookalike is a good mid-range smartphone for a decent price, but not as great as the hype had us believe
  • This traditional Chinese medicine treatment boosts immunity against winter ills like colds, flu, allergies and respiratory illness
  • Indian ‘silver’ divorces rise, with couples more like ‘roommates than soul mates’
  • Meet Prince Philip of Serbia, the bitcoin buff: the American-born son of Alexander, crown prince of Yugoslavia, once worked at The Ritz London and is a vocal crypto advocate
  • Meet Anastasia ‘Stassie’ Karanikolaou, Kylie Jenner’s best friend: she’s a fashion influencer who’s modelled for Rihanna’s Savage x Fenty … but what if that Kardashian fan event never happened?
  • Who is Huma Abedin, Bradley Cooper’s rumoured new romance? She’s Hillary Clinton’s right-hand woman, an author, and was married to disgraced US politician Anthony Weiner
  • 16 richest actors in Marvel’s Thor franchise – net worths, ranked: from Chris Hemsworth and Natalie Portman, of course, to Christian Bale and Scarlett Johansson, but who tops the list?
  • How Melania Trump is fighting back in 2022: the former US first lady has critiqued Joe Biden’s America, stood up to hubby Donald, and even taken a jab at Vogue’s Anna Wintour
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • EDITORIAL: Only radical reform will keep JCP politically relevant
  • VOX POPULI: July 16, the 77th anniversary of the world’s first atomic test
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 17 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Taipei Times: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
